claim_summary: Chapter 6 is a physical-evidence inventory and does not infer ownership, motive, cause of death, or significance beyond documented evidence.
The physical-evidence layer contains 34 catalogue entries, 14 scene components, 34 custody entries, 10 preservation entries, and 10 unresolved questions.
The catalogue covers body/scene, clothing, pocket property, tickets, suitcase, Tamam Shud and Rubaiyat material, forensic identifiers, environmental observations, and preservation records.
No physical-evidence entry has a complete custody trail; 29 are fragmentary, 4 unknown, and 1 not applicable.
Table summarizes catalogue, scene, custody, preservation, and uncertainty register counts.
The body location and orientation are recorded near Somerton Beach seawall with head/shoulders supported, head inclined right, feet toward sea/west, and arm positions described.
The partly smoked cigarette is recorded as a scene/body-adjacent observation, not a recovered and tested exhibit.
Distance and environmental information remain incomplete; no original scene sketch was acquired.
Scene documentation is limited because original scene-photo set, precise measurements, and scene diagram are not preserved locally as publication-cleared materials.
No publication-cleared physical-evidence image was inserted; placeholder records rights/custody limitation.
PE-0003 records body clothing ensemble mapped to EV-0005; current location and survival status unknown.
PE-0004 records body and suitcase tie evidence including T. Keane reference, without resolving ownership or survival.
PE-0005 supports shoe existence and condition observations without gait, occupation, travel, or status inference.
PE-0006 records socks and associated vegetation uncertainty; exact type, condition, retention, and vegetation preservation unknown.
PE-0007 records missing/removed labels while preserving Cleland limitation on recency; no timing, intent, or concealment conclusion is drawn.
PE-0008 and PE-0009 record rail and bus tickets, but not purchaser identity or full route travelled.
PE-0002 and PE-0010 separate observed cigarette placement from cigarette packet inventory, with no confirmed saliva, fingerprint, brand-comparison, or toxicological examination.
PE-0011, PE-0012, and PE-0013 are inventory items only and do not support behavioural inference.
PE-0033 records a low-confidence OCR-dependent 2s 6d cash entry requiring visual confirmation.
PE-0017 records unclaimed suitcase, Leane 1949-01-14 location, police possession 1949-01-19, and PE-0018 luggage ticket; depositor identity unknown.
Suitcase evidence supports existence, police possession, and internal association evidence, not ownership, complete custody, complete inventory, or current survival.
PE-0019 records suitcase clothing and textiles with moderate confidence due OCR distortion and missing verified layout.
PE-0020 records suitcase tools and personal articles with limited examination, without occupational inference.
PE-0021 records six pencils or writing implements, without establishing relation to the Rubaiyat code or technical occupation.
PE-0022 records warm sepia/orange/tan thread correspondence testimony but not ownership proof or retained sample status.
PE-0014 records Tamam Shud slip existence, fob-pocket association, paper inquiry, and exhibit handling.
Slip custody is fragmentary; exact discovery date/time and finder sequence unresolved.
Known examination is limited to textual/paper comparison and inquiries; no acquired fibre, chemical, or modern physical report; current physical status unknown.
PE-0015 records Brown comparison and coroner association with second edition Fitzgerald Rubaiyat.
Associated original book recovery, owner/finder, edition, tear-match, and current location remain unresolved.
PE-0016 records local digital code image but original book/police custody unresolved; code analysis deferred.
verification_status: Verified digital copy; original custody unresolved

claim_summary: PE-0025 records fingerprints and local digital reproduction; original chart and bureau replies not acquired.
PE-0026 records dental chart and local reproduction; exact dental notation requires specialist/manual transcription.
PE-0027 records death mask/bust creation and exhibit marking; current custody low and appearance inference limited by embalming/postmortem change.
PE-0028 hair has weak primary support as preserved exhibit in acquired 1949 material; later hair/exhumation claims require official source, custody, and lab records.
PE-0029 records biological specimens submitted to Cowan; present availability unknown and no retention/disposal record acquired.
Custody matrix contains 34 entries: 29 fragmentary, 4 unknown, 1 not applicable, and no complete custody trail.
Fragmentary custody separates documented existence/observation from incomplete later movement, storage, transfer, destruction, or custodian records.
Unknown custody applies to PE-0023, PE-0024, PE-0028, and PE-0033.
Table summarizes complete, mostly complete, fragmentary, unknown, lost, and not-applicable custody counts.

Preservation register contains 10 entries with three local digital copies, two reference-only photo records, unverified death-mask status, unknown suitcase/clothing/slip status, and likely unavailable but unverified biological specimens.
Preservation register is treated as acquisition guide and separates inspection availability from exhibit custody.
Table records PRH-0001 through PRH-0010 preservation status.
Strongest physical-evidence records are those mapped to testimony, inquest pages, exhibit references, or preserved reproductions.
Weakest physical-evidence records are those with incomplete custody, exact wording, or primary-source support.

Principal missing evidence consists of custody and preservation records for photographs, clothing, property, suitcase, Rubaiyat provenance, slip discovery, thread/labels, biological specimens, and current custodians.
Complete physical evidence inventory lists PE-0001 through PE-0034 with evidence IDs, descriptions, custody, and confidence.
Known missing or unlocated evidence table records key PRH preservation/custody gaps.
Evidence uncertainty table records PEU-0001 through PEU-0010 and priority levels.
